info.title: repomngr
info.description: Resource Manager 2.0 is a part of the Coscine group.

commands.name: cleanbranches
commands.description: Delete older branches from a project.

options.name: --age
options.description: An argument that provides the maximum age in months of a branch since its last activity and classifies it as old. Example: "--age 6" will delete all branches that have not had any commit activity in the past 6 months.

options.name: --blacklist
options.description: An argument that provides a regex blacklist pattern for branch names. All branches with names that conform to the provided regex pattern will be SKIPPED. All others will be worked on.

options.name: --dummy
options.description: An argument that tells the program to execute in dummy mode. No actual branches will be deleted.

options.name: --groups
options.description: Comma-separated list of groups. All subgroups are automatically included. Example: "coscine/backend, coscine/frontend, coscine/graphs, coscine/tools, coscine/x"

options.name: --merged
options.description: An argument that tells the program to only delete merged branches. All branches that have not been merged, will be ignored.

options.name: --projects
options.description: Comma-separated list of GitLab project IDs. Will update the information only for the projects provided. Example: "31552, 41189, 32025"

options.name: --whitelist
options.description: An argument that provides a regex whitelist pattern for branch names. All branches with names that conform to the provided regex pattern will be WORKED ON. All others will be skipped.

commands.name: clone
commands.description: An argument that tells the program to clone a GitLab repository.

options.name: --branch
options.description: Branch to switch to. Default on switching is the current local repository project branch. Default on cloning will be the default project branch (e.g. "master" or "main").

options.name: --clean
options.description: An argument that tells the program to clean all unstaged changes in the local repository.

options.name: --destination
options.description: Path where the locally cloned repositories should be located.

options.name: --dummy
options.description: An argument that tells the program to execute in dummy mode. No folders will be created.

options.name: --email
options.description: GitLab user email to use for cloning.

options.name: --groups
options.description: Comma-separated list of groups. All subgroups are automatically included. If this value is not provided, it defaults to "coscine/backend, coscine/frontend, coscine/graphs, coscine/tools, coscine/x"

options.name: --hard
options.description: An argument that tells the program to reset and discard all current changes in the work tree before switching branch.

options.name: --pipeline
options.description: An argument that tells the program that the execution is running inside a pipeline. Cloning safe-guards will be disabled.

options.name: --token
options.description: GitLab token to use for cloning.

options.name: --user
options.description: GitLab user name to use for cloning.

commands.name: consulkeys
commands.description: Deploy Consul Keys to the machine from a provided JSON file.

options.name: --file
options.description: An argument that points to the json file with content to deploy.

options.name: --backup
options.description: An argument that allows for the creation of a backup of all Consul keys to be overwritten.

options.name: --blacklist
options.description: Comma-separated list of forbidden key paths. Forbid the deletion and edit of all Consul keys starting with this path, so the program will not touch them. If key is not present in Consul it will be set regardless. Example: "coscine/global/db_name, coscine/global/gitlabtoken"

options.name: --no-dummy
options.description: An argument that tells the program to execute in dummy mode. No keys will be deleted, backed up, replaced or added.

options.name: --overwrite
options.description: An argument that tells the program to overwrite all keys inside Consul, while taking the whitelist into consideration. If this argument is NOT provided the program will only add missing keys inside Consul and existing keys won't be touched regardless of their value.

options.name: --redeploy
options.description: A redeploy argument which removes all Consul keys from your system and deploys them again. Touched are ONLY keys with a path contained in the whitelist. If the whitelist is empty on redeploy, ALL keys will be deleted. A backup will be forced.

options.name: --whitelist
options.description: Comma-separated list of allowed key paths. Limit the scope of the application only to the Consul keys starting with this path. Every other path not starting with any of the provided values will be IGNORED. Example: "coscine/global, coscine/local"

commands.name: devbranch
commands.description: Create a "dev" branch in every project.

options.name: --dummy
options.description: An argument that tells the program to execute in dummy mode. No actual dev branches will be created.

options.name: --groups
options.description: Comma-separated list of groups. All subgroups are automatically included. Example: "coscine/backend, coscine/frontend, coscine/graphs, coscine/tools, coscine/x"

options.name: --projects
options.description: Comma-separated list of GitLab project IDs. Will update the information only for the projects provided. Example: "31552, 41189, 32025"

options.name: --update
options.description: An argument that tells the program to execute in update mode. All protected branches will first be unprotected and then protected again with the settings provided.

commands.name: devrebase
commands.description: Rebase target branch using the source branch. Should a rebase have conflicts, then a manual intervetion will be neccessary and the program will skip to the next repository.

options.name: --target
options.description: Target branch name to rebase.

options.name: --destination
options.description: Path where the locally cloned repositories should be located. Local repositories are required to initiate rebase.

options.name: --dummy
options.description: An argument that tells the program to execute in dummy mode. No actual branches will be created.

options.name: --email
options.description: GitLab user email to use for cloning.

options.name: --force
options.description: An argument that tells the program to execute git commands with the force flag.

options.name: --source
options.description: Source branch name to use for the rebase of the target branch. Commits from this branch will land on the target branch. If no value is provided, will use the default project branch (e.g. master or main).

options.name: --token
options.description: GitLab token to use for the GitLab client.

options.name: --user
options.description: GitLab user name to use for cloning.

commands.name: devsnapshot
commands.description: Create new snapshot branch from the "dev" branch in every project.

options.name: --iteration
options.description: An argument that points to the iteration to be used.

options.name: --name
options.description: Name of the branch snapshot created from the "dev" branch (e.g. "Sprint/xxxx-xx").

options.name: --dummy
options.description: An argument that tells the program to execute in dummy mode. No actual branches will be created.

options.name: --groups
options.description: Comma-separated list of groups. All subgroups are automatically included. Example: "coscine/backend, coscine/frontend, coscine/graphs, coscine/tools, coscine/x"

options.name: --projects
options.description: Comma-separated list of GitLab project IDs. Will update the information only for the projects provided. Example: "31552, 41189, 32025"

commands.name: finrelease
commands.description: Prepare the Release. This implies creation of Sprint branches and necessary Merge Requests.

options.name: --assignee-usernames
options.description: Comma-separated list of GitLab usernames to set as assignees.

options.name: --dummy
options.description: An argument that tells the program to execute in dummy mode. No actual Merge Requests or branches will be created.

options.name: --iteration
options.description: An argument that points to the iteration to be used.

options.name: --label-closed
options.description: An argument that tells the program to label all closed issues from an iteration.

options.name: --reviewer-usernames
options.description: Comma-separated list of GitLab usernames to set as reviewers.

commands.name: integrations
commands.description: Generate the Integrator Script's "integrations.json" for the release.

options.name: --branch
options.description: Branch name to generate integrations.json for. If not provided, then it defaults to the latest project tag.

options.name: --dummy
options.description: An argument that tells the program to execute in dummy mode. Will only simulate a successful execution.

options.name: --outdir
options.description: An argument that sets the output destination directory for the json file.

commands.name: mergerules
commands.description: Replace all existing Project Merge Rules inside GitLab with a new Merge Rule.

options.name: --appr-group
options.description: Comma-separated list of GitLab group IDs to get Merge Request approval access.

options.name: --appr-usernames
options.description: Comma-separated list of GitLab usernames to get Merge Request approval access.

options.name: --groups
options.description: Comma-separated list of GitLab groups. Will update the information in every project inside the groups provided. Example: "coscine/backend, coscine/frontend"

options.name: --projects
options.description: Comma-separated list of GitLab project IDs. Will update the information only for the projects provided. Example: "31552, 41189, 32025"

options.name: --title
options.description: Approval rule title. Example: "Merging into master/main"

options.name: --approvals
options.description: Amount of approvals required to be able to merge.

options.name: --branch
options.description: Protected branch name to apply the Merge Request rule to. It defaults to the default project branch (e.g. master or main). If this value is equal to "//ANY", the program will apply the rule to ANY branch in the project.

options.name: --dummy
options.description: An argument that tells the program to execute in dummy mode. No actual Merge Requests rules will be created or deleted.

commands.name: preprelease
commands.description: Prepare the Release. This implies creation of Sprint branches and necessary Merge Requests.

options.name: --all
options.description: Process all issues found in the iteration. Extract branch names automatically.

options.name: --branch
options.description: Comma-separated list of branches to be used.

options.name: --issuenr
options.description: An argument that points to the GitLab issue number to be used.

options.name: --dummy
options.description: An argument that tells the program to execute in dummy mode. No actual Merge Requests or branches will be created.

options.name: --iteration
options.description: An argument that points to the iteration to be used.

options.name: --mrtitle
options.description: An argument that points to the Merge Request title to be used.

commands.name: projectbadge
commands.description: Create a "dev" branch in every project.

options.name: --dummy
options.description: An argument that tells the program to execute in dummy mode. No actual badges will be created.

options.name: --projects
options.description: Comma-separated list of GitLab project IDs. Will update the information only for the projects provided. Example: "31552, 41189, 32025"
